    "R1.2": "The paper asserts a self-supervised tool-selection mechanism but describes a fixed WFST-constrained beam search with a static lexicon FST and a hard OOL penalty — a potential disconnect between the learning claim and the deterministic implementation warrants logical scrutiny.",
    "R1.3": "WER figures (2.8% test-clean, 5.9%→8.1% rare-word delta, 14.2%→9.7% medical) are present but the artifact provides no ablation isolating the tool-invocation mechanism from the lexicon FST alone; evidence-claim alignment is therefore a plausible primary concern.",

  "clarification_question": "Is your primary concern (a) whether the scientific claims and reported evidence hold together (R1.2 + R1.3 as load-bearing), or (b) whether the contribution framing and novelty over standard WFST-constrained CTC is justified (R1.1 + R1.4 as load-bearing)?"

rationale.R1.1: Any general opinion on a research artifact must engage with the thesis (self-supervised tool augmentation for OOV ASR) and contribution claim; the framing of WFST decoding as 'learning to invoke tools' may itself be the central point of contention.
rationale.R1.2: The paper asserts a self-supervised tool-selection mechanism but describes a fixed WFST-constrained beam search with a static lexicon FST and a hard OOL penalty — a potential disconnect between the learning claim and the deterministic implementation warrants logical scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: WER figures (2.8% test-clean, 5.9%→8.1% rare-word delta, 14.2%→9.7% medical) are present but the artifact provides no ablation isolating the tool-invocation mechanism from the lexicon FST alone; evidence-claim alignment is therefore a plausible primary concern.
rationale.R1.4: WFST-lexicon-constrained CTC decoding with CMUdict is well-established; whether augmenting the FST with mined named entities constitutes the 'self-supervised tool augmentation' novelty claimed is a plausible load-bearing question.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary concern (a) whether the scientific claims and reported evidence hold together (R1.2 + R1.3 as load-bearing), or (b) whether the contribution framing and novelty over standard WFST-constrained CTC is justified (R1.1 + R1.4 as load-bearing)?
